response.justification: Candidate has broad full-stack experience spanning 15+ years with recent Staff-level title at re:cinq and exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which aligns with the AI-native engineering environment. However, the resume shows no demonstrable Python expertise (a must-have), and distributed/high-throughput systems experience is limited primarily to one role at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (2018-2020) using DLang rather than the required stack. Strong TypeScript/Node.js background and current AI-agent tooling work justify a conversation, but the Python gap and limited recent distributed-systems depth are significant.
strengths: Current Staff role with hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generator experience
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background plus polyglot breadth across web, mobile, embedded
Demonstrated craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, workshops on testing and web internals
concerns: No evidence of strong Python skills despite being a must-have requirement
High-throughput distributed systems experience is dated (2018-2020) and was in DLang, not Python
No explicit mention of on-call rotation, incident response, or large-scale operational ownership

key_factors.factor: AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience
